title: Commodity Update – March 2026
summary: Benchmark aluminum prices surged 1.7% on the LME due to Middle East conflict concerns disrupting supply from a region producing 9% of global aluminum. Emirates Global Aluminum and Century Aluminum announced a joint venture for a new production facility in Oklahoma to boost U.S. capacity. The conflict risks higher prices and supply chain issues unless resolved.

title: Morgan Stanley Forecasts $3,700/Tonne Aluminum Price for 2026
summary: Morgan Stanley forecasts aluminum prices reaching $3,700 per tonne by 2026 amid Middle East supply disruptions, including smelter shutdowns totaling 564,000 tonnes annually due to Strait of Hormuz constraints. The region, accounting for 9% of global production, faces feedstock shortages from imported alumina and bauxite. Additional factors include constrained Chinese supply and slow Indonesian expansion.

title: Alcoa draws new aluminum orders as Gulf states curb production
summary: Alcoa, the largest U.S. aluminum producer, reports increased orders and inquiries as Gulf states curtail production—about 9% of global supply—due to Strait of Hormuz closure and raw material shortages. Alcoa's alumina shipments originally destined for the Middle East are redirecting elsewhere, like China. Aluminum prices hit highs since 2022, with U.S. Midwest premium at a record $1.10 per pound.

title: Aluminium prices surge, supplies to America and Europe threatened
summary: Aluminum prices are surging due to the closure of Qatar's Qatalum smelter from gas disruptions, threatening global supplies particularly to America and Europe. This adds to regional production challenges amid geopolitical tensions.

title: Strait of Hormuz closure sparks revised aluminium price forecast, pushing it above $4000/t
summary: ING Group revised its aluminum price forecast above $4,000 per tonne due to the Strait of Hormuz closure and escalating Middle East geopolitical conflict impacting production capacity.

title: Rio Tinto, Prysmian Team Up for Green Data Center Aluminum
summary: Rio Tinto and Prysmian have partnered on an industrial trial to produce low-carbon aluminium cables for data centers, using aluminium from Rio Tinto's hydropowered Alma smelter and ELYSIS technology that eliminates direct greenhouse gas emissions. This combines leading-edge smelting and cable technologies to reduce the carbon footprint of IT infrastructure. The companies signed a five-year supply agreement in 2023 to accelerate lower-carbon aluminium solutions.

title: Aluminium Giants Hit Major Milestone with Low-Carbon Production
summary: Elysis, a joint venture of Alcoa and Rio Tinto, achieved a breakthrough by installing inert anode technology in a 450-kA cell, scaling up from a 100-kA pilot that produced low-carbon aluminium for Apple products, Michelob Ultra cans, and Audi wheels. This carbon-free technology replaces the 140-year-old smelting process, eliminating CO2 and PFCs. The milestone demonstrates viability at full-scale smelter levels.

title: Constellium Releases 2025 Sustainability Report, Shaping the ...
summary: Constellium's 2025 report highlights a 16% reduction in emissions intensity through increased recycling, with scrap input reaching 47% of total metal input, up 13% from 2024. Scope 1 and 2 emissions intensity improved 14%, surpassing targets via renewable electricity and energy-efficient tech. The company advanced low-carbon alloys and closed-loop partnerships for greater product circularity.

title: Recycled AL & Sustainability update: Plant fire, low-carbon cable ...
summary: Recent updates cover fire disruptions at plants, low-carbon cable trials, recycling gains, and major global expansions in the aluminium sector. These developments emphasize sustainability efforts amid challenges. The report highlights ongoing innovations in recycled aluminium and low-carbon production.

title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Steel prices fell to 3,137 CNY/T on March 18, 2026, down 0.70% from the previous day, while steel rebar futures held steady around CNY 3,140 per ton near seven-week highs. Chinese steel output dropped 3.6% to 160 million tons in the first two months due to curtailed production amid weak demand and seasonal slowdowns from Lunar New Year and pollution controls. Over the past month, prices rose 3.43%, though still 0.79% lower year-over-year.

title: Stocks of main finished steel products in China up 7.0% in early March 2026
summary: Domestic inventories of five main finished steel products in 21 major Chinese cities reached 11.64 million mt as of March 10, up 7.0% or 0.76 million mt from February 28. Inventories of HRC, CRC, wire rod, and rebar increased by 2.5%, 4.1%, 6.4%, and 12.9% respectively, while medium steel plate inventories fell 1.5%. This buildup reflects ongoing market dynamics in early March.

title: Global race as market for high-grade iron ore grows
summary: Global demand for high-grade iron ores is rising as the steel sector pursues decarbonization, leading to a looming supply deficit. This trend intensifies competition in the market for premium iron ore grades essential for lower-emission steel production.

title: Market Update: March 2026
summary: Domestic steel pricing continues to climb above $1,000 per ton amid ongoing trade restrictions that limit access to North American supply outside the United States. These factors contribute to sustained upward pressure on steel market prices.

title: U.S. Steel Production Gains Despite Weekly Dip: AISI
summary: U.S. domestic raw steel production totaled 1.774 million net tons for the week ending March 14, 2026, with a capacity utilization rate of 76.7%, down slightly from the prior week but up 4.2% year-over-year. Year-to-date production through March 14 reached 18.613 million net tons at 77.2% utilization, a 4.9% increase from 2025. Regional output was led by the Southern district at 795,000 net tons.

title: Primetals Technologies Delivers Fourth Continuous Caster to HBIS Tangsteel
summary: HBIS Tangsteel has started up a new slab caster, its fourth continuous caster from Primetals Technologies, at the Tangshan Laoting plant in Hebei Province, China. This addition enhances the plant's steel production capacity through advanced continuous casting technology.

title: Global high-grade iron ore market is set to grow
summary: The global high-grade iron ore market is projected to expand, potentially impacting low-emissions steel production. DR-grade iron ore could become a constraint as steelmakers shift toward greener processes requiring higher-quality inputs.

title: Future of Steel: Trends, Technology & Sustainability 2026
summary: The article explores upcoming trends in steel production, focusing on sustainability through direct reduced iron (DRI) technology, hydrogen-based operations, molten oxide electrolysis, and enhanced steel scrap recycling. These innovations aim to create a circular steel economy with minimal waste and low emissions, aligning with global climate goals. By 2026, scrap-based manufacturing and AI-driven scrap sorting are expected to drive reliability and low-carbon steel markets.[1]

title: Tata Steel and USTB Forge Path Toward Low-Carbon Steelmaking
summary: Tata Steel has signed an MoU with the University of Science and Technology Beijing (USTB) to collaborate on low-carbon steelmaking technologies. The partnership focuses on scrap-based steelmaking, steel waste valorization, end-product performance, and carbon capture, leveraging USTB's facilities to pilot scalable solutions. This initiative supports the global transition to sustainable steel production.[2]

title: SandLix™ reaches major milestone on the path towards enabling more sustainable copper production
summary: Anglo American's SandLix™ heap leach process has reached a major milestone in addressing copper industry challenges like orebody depletion, water scarcity, and energy consumption. It supports sustainable copper production amid rising demand from energy transition and urbanization. The innovation directly tackles pressing issues for future copper supply.

title: Copper in the Age of AI: Challenges of Electrification
summary: S&P Global analyzes copper supply and demand through 2040, highlighting its role in AI data centers, electrification, and energy transition. Demand will grow from 28 million metric tons in 2025 to 42 million by 2040, driven by Asia, with needs for accelerated mining, new technologies, and expanded processing. AI and data centers emerge as key demand drivers due to high electricity intensity and copper use.

title: Copper Prices Surge Above $13,000: Best Copper Stocks to Watch in 2026
summary: Copper prices exceed $13,000 due to AI data centers and electrification boosting demand, with EVs, renewables, and grids requiring more copper. A single AI facility may need 50,000 metric tons, and data centers could demand 475,000 tons in 2026. Supply challenges persist, prompting investments in production and low-carbon copper deals.

title: Ten major mining tech trends in 2026: Part 1
summary: Mining tech trends focus on reprocessing copper waste, with Freeport-McMoRan targeting 800 million pounds annually from stockpiles using new leaching technologies. Innovations include processing e-waste for multiple metals and reducing water use in copper production by up to 78% via advanced flowsheets. These efforts unlock value from waste and enhance sustainability.

title: Nickel Prices Rebounded on March 18
summary: Nickel prices rebounded on March 18, 2026, with market participants noting that the US Fed may be more inclined to remain silent this week. This rebound follows earlier price retreats and reflects ongoing sensitivity to macroeconomic factors and monetary policy expectations.

title: Why copper and nickel prices are moving today: key market drivers
summary: As of March 16, nickel was trading at $17,428/tonne, up 0.62% on the day, supported by Indonesia's supply restraint and tighter ore availability. Nickel has risen 3.55% over the past month and 6.20% year-over-year, showing stronger medium-term performance despite recent volatility.

title: 3-Month Nickel Commodity Futures Price Drops to US$17530/ton
summary: The 3-month nickel commodity futures price declined to US$17,530 per ton on March 13, 2026, down from US$17,725 the previous day. Year-to-date, nickel prices have risen 3.64% from the start-of-year level of US$16,915 per ton, with the highest monthly average recorded in January 2026 at US$18,030 per ton.
